sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After clicking on the green+c at the right frame:
- Bottom bar stayed at 10 pixels (didn't extend further)
- Centers swapped: left center became c (was W), right center became W (was c)
So clicking on the green+c at the right frame DID swap the colors (c↔W), but the bottom bar didn't extend. This is different from clicking the green+W at the left frame, which extended the bar but didn't swap colors.
Wait, let me reconsider. The green+c was at the right frame position. When I clicked it:
- Centers swapped: left c, right W
- Bottom bar stayed at 10 pixels
But earlier, when I clicked the green+W at the left frame:
- Centers stayed the same (left W, right c)
- Bottom bar extended from 9 to 10
Hmm, but wait. Before the left frame click, the centers were left W, right c. After the click, they were still left W, right c. So no swap.
After the right frame click, centers went from left W, right c to left c, right W. So a swap happened.
This is confusing. Let me think about what's different:
1. Click green+W at left frame: no swap, bar extended
2. Click green+c at right frame: swap, bar didn't extend
Maybe the mechanic is:
- Clicking a green square at a frame position "activates" it if its center color matches the frame's interior color
- The left frame has w (light gray) interior, and the green+W had W (white) center - close but not exact match
- The right frame has w (light gray) interior, and the green+c had c (charcoal) center - no match
Or maybe:
- Clicking a green square at a frame position with a WHITE center extends the bar
- Clicking a green square at a frame position with a non-white center swaps colors
Let me test this hypothesis. Currently:
- Left green: center c (at left frame)
- Right green: center W (at right frame)
If I click the right green (center W, at right frame), it should extend the bar.
If I click the left green (center c, at left frame), it should swap colors.
Let me try clicking the right green (center W) at the right frame.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Clicking green+c at right frame swapped colors (left now c, right now W) but bar stayed at 10. Previously clicking green+W at left frame extended bar but didn't swap. Hypothesis: clicking a green square with WHITE center at a frame extends the progress bar, while clicking non-white swaps colors. Let me test by clicking the right green (now W center) at the right frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on the green+W at the right frame (center W at row 31, col 43)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 43}])
print("Result:", result)

# Check centers and bottom bar
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("\nCenters and bar:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"  Center: id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W' and 8 <= n['pixels'] <= 15:
        print(f"  Bar: id={n['id']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
